outcome_text: Organizational risk tolerances are determined and documented.
scoring_text: Skill in determining and documenting organizational AI risk tolerance, including conducting risk appetite assessments, defining quantitative and qualitative risk thresholds for AI systems, and establishing documented risk tolerance statements that guide AI development and deployment decisions.
_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Skill in determining and documenting organizational AI risk tolerance, including conducting risk appetite assessments, defining quantitative and qualitative risk thresholds for AI systems, and establishing documented risk tolerance statements that guide AI development and deployment decisions.
reference_document_element_description: Skill in designing risk appetite statements for data and AI activities that are specific enough to guide operational risk decisions yet flexible enough to accommodate business evolution and emerging use cases.
comments: NIST AI RMF MP-1.5 (MAP) equal WIDAI RM domain. RM-S-008 is the strongest match (STS: 0.744, cosine: 0.740). Secondary: RC-S-008 (RC, cosine: 0.708), AG-S-005 (AG, cosine: 0.707).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression. Primary method for all elements. Scores the competency_implication text (workforce competency interpretation of the risk management outcome) against WIDAI KSA statements.
primary_method.significance: STS 7.4/10 (raw 0.744). Shared vocabulary: [ai, appetite, decisions, guide, risk, skill, statements]. NIST AI RMF competency implication terms [assessments, conducting, defining, deployment, determining, development] vs WIDAI terms [accommodate, activities, business, cases, data, designing] define the semantic distance.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.891 (P=0.898, R=0.885). Balanced P/R — the 36-word competency implication and 31-word WIDAI KSA cover each other's token-level semantic content symmetrically.

nli_cross_encoder.description: Natural Language Inference classification. NLI entailment is systematically low for competency→competency pairs because NLI measures strict logical implication, not semantic relatedness. Primary diagnostic value is in detecting contradiction.
nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (99.9%). Standard result for cross-framework competency mapping — the NIST AI RMF element and WIDAI KSA describe overlapping competencies without strict logical implication.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.740 ≈ STS 0.744 (gap 0.004). Independent encoding and cross-attention agree. Shared terms [ai, appetite, decisions, guide, risk] are sufficient for independent encoding to detect the match.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 25.6%. Shared tokens: [ai, appetite, decisions, guide, risk, skill, statements]. NIST AI RMF-only: [assessments, conducting, defining, deployment, determining, development]. WIDAI-only: [accommodate, activities, business, cases, data, designing].
